meta.source_paper: L2Beat sequencer analysis, Espresso/Astria shared sequencer research

risk_domain.name: Sequencer-Specific Privacy Risks
risk_domain.description: Privacy risks arising from centralized and shared L2 sequencers. RPC guard that detects sequencer-level threats: transaction content leakage, censorship, MEV extraction, and cross-rollup correlation. Extends l2_anonymity_set H2.

capabilities: full visibility of all transactions before batch inclusion (sequencer operator)
ability to reorder transactions within a batch for MEV extraction
cross-rollup correlation when operating as shared sequencer
selective censorship of specific addresses or transaction types
timing analysis of transaction submission patterns
limitations: cannot forge transactions or modify calldata
L1 forced inclusion provides eventual censorship resistance

H1_centralized_sequencer.name: Centralized sequencer
H1_centralized_sequencer.description: Target L2 operates a single centralized sequencer run by one entity. This operator sees all transactions before they are batched, gaining full visibility into transaction content, ordering, and sender identity pre-publication.

signals.name: single_operator
signals.description: L2 sequencer is operated by a single entity with no rotation or decentralization mechanism

signals.name: no_encrypted_mempool
signals.description: L2 does not implement encrypted mempool or commit-reveal for transaction content

signals.name: sequencer_ip_visible
signals.description: Transactions submitted directly to sequencer endpoint expose sender IP without Tor/VPN

detection.threshold: single_operator AND no_encrypted_mempool triggers WARNING for privacy-sensitive txs

recommendations.description: Submit through a privacy-preserving relay or encrypted mempool if available on the L2
Check sequencer decentralization roadmap and prefer L2s with active decentralization progress

H1_centralized_sequencer.fundamental_limitation: Until sequencer decentralization is achieved, the sequencer operator inherently has god-mode visibility. No client-side mitigation fully eliminates this trust assumption.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Survey of N L2 networks with known sequencer architectures
benchmark_scenario.metric: accuracy of sequencer centralization classification across all surveyed L2s
benchmark_scenario.baseline: as of 2024, most major L2s run centralized sequencers (L2Beat data)

H2_sequencer_censorship.name: Sequencer censorship
H2_sequencer_censorship.description: Transaction is not included by the sequencer despite valid gas and formatting. May indicate selective censorship based on sender address, destination, or transaction content. Privacy risk because censorship reveals the sequencer is profiling transactions.

signals.name: inclusion_delay
signals.description: Transaction not included within expected time window despite sufficient gas and valid nonce

signals.name: selective_pattern
signals.description: Other transactions from same block with lower gas were included while this one was skipped

signals.name: repeated_exclusion
signals.description: Same sender address has been excluded from multiple consecutive batches

detection.threshold: repeated_exclusion triggers ALERT, selective_pattern triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Submit transaction directly to L1 forced inclusion queue to bypass sequencer entirely
Track inclusion time and flag anomalous delays that may indicate censorship

H2_sequencer_censorship.fundamental_limitation: Distinguishing censorship from congestion or priority fee sorting is inherently ambiguous. Short-term non-inclusion may be legitimate; only persistent patterns are indicative.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Submit N transactions across L2s with varying gas prices, measure inclusion times
benchmark_scenario.metric: ability to distinguish censorship from congestion based on inclusion patterns
benchmark_scenario.baseline: documented censorship incidents rare but increasing as L2 usage grows

H3_sequencer_mev.name: Sequencer MEV extraction
H3_sequencer_mev.description: Sequencer reorders transactions within a batch to extract MEV. Unlike L1 where block builders compete, a centralized sequencer has monopoly power over ordering and can sandwich or front-run with zero competition.

signals.name: suspicious_reordering
signals.description: Transaction ordering in batch does not match submission order or gas priority

signals.name: sequencer_adjacent_profit
signals.description: Sequencer-controlled address profits from transactions adjacent to user's in batch

recommendations.description: Prefer L2s that implement FCFS or other fair ordering mechanisms
Compare submission order to inclusion order to detect suspicious reordering patterns

H3_sequencer_mev.fundamental_limitation: Sequencer MEV is inherent to centralized ordering. Only decentralized sequencing, encrypted mempools, or verifiable ordering constraints can structurally prevent it.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Monitor N batches across L2s, compare submission order to inclusion order
benchmark_scenario.metric: frequency of profitable reordering by sequencer vs fair FCFS ordering
benchmark_scenario.baseline: sequencer MEV on L2s is largely unquantified due to opacity

H4_shared_sequencer_linkage.name: Shared sequencer linkage
H4_shared_sequencer_linkage.description: A shared sequencer operating across multiple rollups can correlate user activity cross-rollup. If the same entity sequences transactions on Rollup A and Rollup B, they can link addresses that transact on both based on timing, amounts, and behavioral patterns.

signals.name: shared_sequencer_detected
signals.description: Target L2 uses a shared sequencer that also sequences for other rollups the user interacts with

signals.name: cross_rollup_timing
signals.description: User transactions on multiple rollups sharing a sequencer show correlated timing patterns

signals.name: amount_correlation
signals.description: Transfer amounts across rollups suggest same-entity bridging through shared sequencer view

recommendations.description: Use L2s with different sequencer operators for activities you want to keep unlinkable
Introduce random delays between cross-rollup transactions to break timing linkage

H4_shared_sequencer_linkage.fundamental_limitation: Shared sequencers are designed for atomic cross-rollup operations, which inherently requires visibility across rollups. The privacy cost is architectural, not a bug.

benchmark_scenario.setup: User operates on N rollups with shared vs independent sequencers, adversary attempts correlation
benchmark_scenario.metric: adversary accuracy in linking cross-rollup addresses via shared sequencer data
benchmark_scenario.baseline: shared sequencer correlation is theoretical -- no large-scale empirical data yet

H5_preconfirmation_privacy.name: Pre-confirmation privacy leak
H5_preconfirmation_privacy.description: Sequencer publishes soft confirmations (pre-confirmations) that reveal transaction content before the batch is posted to L1. This gives observers a time window to act on private information before it becomes part of the canonical chain.

signals.name: preconf_enabled
signals.description: L2 publishes pre-confirmations that include full transaction details before L1 posting

signals.name: preconf_to_l1_gap
signals.description: Time gap between pre-confirmation and L1 batch posting exceeds 10 minutes

signals.name: preconf_public_api
signals.description: Pre-confirmation data is accessible via public API without authentication

recommendations.description: Avoid time-sensitive transactions on L2s with long pre-confirmation windows
Track pre-confirmation to L1 posting delay to assess information leakage window

H5_preconfirmation_privacy.fundamental_limitation: Pre-confirmations are a UX feature that trades privacy for speed. The information leakage is by design -- faster confirmation requires earlier content revelation.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Monitor pre-confirmation feeds across N L2s, measure content exposure vs L1 finality gap
benchmark_scenario.metric: information leakage window (time between content-revealing preconf and L1 inclusion)
benchmark_scenario.baseline: pre-confirmation windows range from seconds to minutes depending on L2

sequencer_monitor.description: Monitor sequencer ordering patterns, detect reordering and MEV extraction

decentralization_checker.description: Check sequencer operator count, decentralization roadmap status, and shared sequencer usage

inclusion_timer.description: Track transaction submission to inclusion timing, detect anomalous delays suggesting censorship

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring sequencer privacy risk detection across L2 networks

methodology.step_1: Survey sequencer architecture of top N L2 networks by TVL
methodology.step_2: Submit test transactions and measure inclusion timing, ordering fidelity, pre-confirmation exposure
methodology.step_3: Detect censorship patterns via controlled exclusion experiments
methodology.step_4: Measure cross-rollup correlation capability for shared sequencer setups
methodology.step_5: Score each L2 on sequencer privacy risk (centralization, MEV, censorship, linkage, preconf)

combined_benchmark.target: accurate risk classification of L2 sequencer privacy for top 20 L2 networks

adversary_levels.name: passive_observer
adversary_levels.description: Reads public pre-confirmations and sequencer data

adversary_levels.name: active_sequencer
adversary_levels.description: Sequencer operator extracting MEV and censoring

templates.risk_assessment: L2 transaction on {chain_name} triggers {heuristic_id} ({heuristic_name}): {signal_description}. Confidence: {confidence}. Sequencer risk: {risk_level}. Recommendation: {recommendation}.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_txs} L2 transactions across {n_chains} chains. {n_risky} flagged for sequencer privacy risks ({pct_risky}%). Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This reduces sequencer privacy exposure by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
